name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory)
notes:
This Building Block triggers when specific dlls or executables are dropped to specific locations. 

These pairings of files and file locations are known to facilitate UAC bypasses by abusing DLL search ordering:
- BluetoothDiagnosticUtil.dll dropped to "%AppData%\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps"
- osk.exe dropped to "C:\Program Files\Windows Media Player"
- OskSupport.dll dropped to "C:\Program Files\Windows Media Player"
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created

when the event matches ("Filename" like 'BluetoothDiagnosticUtil.dll' and "File Directory" matches '.*AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\WindowsApps') OR ("Filename" matches 'OskSupport.dll|osk.exe' and "File Directory" matches '.*Program Files\\Windows Media Player') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: UAC Bypass - DLL Hijacking (Non-System Directory) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created

AND when the event matches ("Filename" like 'BluetoothDiagnosticUtil.dll' and "File Directory" matches '.*AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\WindowsApps') OR ("Filename" matches 'OskSupport.dll|osk.exe' and "File Directory" matches '.*Program Files\\Windows Media Player') AQL filter query
